categories.name: By Ingredient
categories.description: Research organized by specific botanical ingredients

categories.name: By Life Stage
categories.description: Research relevant to specific life stages

categories.name: By Benefit
categories.description: Research organized by health benefit category

research.title: Ginger and Pregnancy-Related Nausea
research.summary: Meta-analysis of 6 randomized controlled trials examining ginger supplementation for pregnancy-related nausea. Results showed significant improvement in nausea symptoms compared to placebo.
research.citation: Viljoen E, et al. A systematic review and meta-analysis of the effect and safety of ginger in the treatment of pregnancy-associated nausea and vomiting. Nutr J. 2014;13:20.

research.title: Ginger and Gastric Motility
research.summary: Double-blind, randomized trial examining the effect of ginger on gastric emptying and GI symptoms. Ginger significantly accelerated gastric emptying and stimulated antral contractions.
research.citation: Wu KL, et al. Effects of ginger on gastric emptying and motility in healthy humans. Eur J Gastroenterol Hepatol. 2008;20(5):436-40.

research.title: Ashwagandha and Stress Management
research.summary: Prospective, double-blind, randomized, placebo-controlled study examining ashwagandha root extract in adults with chronic stress. Significant improvements in stress scores and serum cortisol levels.
keyFindings: Significant reduction in stress and anxiety scores
Improvements in quality of life measures
research.citation: Chandrasekhar K, et al. A prospective, randomized double-blind, placebo-controlled study of safety and efficacy of a high-concentration full-spectrum extract of ashwagandha root in reducing stress and anxiety in adults. Indian J Psychol Med. 2012;34(3):255-62.

research.title: Ashwagandha and Sleep Quality
research.summary: Double-blind, randomized, placebo-controlled study examining ashwagandha's effect on sleep quality and sleep onset latency in adults with insomnia.
research.citation: Langade D, et al. Efficacy and Safety of Ashwagandha Root Extract in Insomnia and Anxiety: A Double-blind, Randomized, Placebo-controlled Study. Cureus. 2019;11(9):e5797.

research.title: Curcumin and Inflammatory Markers
research.summary: Systematic review and meta-analysis of randomized controlled trials examining curcumin supplementation on inflammatory markers. Significant reductions in CRP and other inflammatory markers observed.
research.citation: Sahebkar A, et al. Effect of curcuminoids on oxidative stress: A systematic review and meta-analysis of randomized controlled trials. J Funct Foods. 2015;18:898-909.

research.title: Shatavari and Lactation Support
research.summary: Randomized, double-blind, placebo-controlled study examining shatavari's galactagogue properties in breastfeeding mothers with insufficient milk production.
keyFindings: No adverse effects on mother or infant
Traditional use supported by clinical evidence
research.citation: Sharma S, et al. A randomized, double-blind, placebo-controlled study to evaluate the galactagogue activity of Shatavari in breastfeeding mothers.

research.title: Moringa Nutritional Profile and Health Benefits
research.summary: Comprehensive review of moringa's nutritional composition and potential health applications, including its rich vitamin, mineral, and antioxidant content.
keyFindings: Rich in vitamins A, C, and E
research.citation: Stohs SJ, Hartman MJ. Review of the Safety and Efficacy of Moringa oleifera. Phytother Res. 2015;29(6):796-804.

research.title: Holy Basil (Tulsi) and Stress Response
research.summary: Double-blind, placebo-controlled study examining the adaptogenic effects of holy basil extract on stress, anxiety, and depression symptoms.
keyFindings: Well-tolerated with no serious adverse events
research.citation: Saxena RC, et al. Efficacy of an Extract of Ocimum tenuiflorum (OciBest) in the Management of General Stress: A Double-Blind, Placebo-Controlled Study. Evid Based Complement Alternat Med. 2012;2012:894509.

evidenceLevels.name: Strong Evidence
evidenceLevels.description: Multiple randomized controlled trials or meta-analyses with consistent findings

evidenceLevels.name: Moderate Evidence
evidenceLevels.description: At least one randomized controlled trial or multiple observational studies

evidenceLevels.name: Emerging Evidence
evidenceLevels.description: Preliminary studies or traditional use with scientific plausibility

glossary.definition: A study where participants are randomly assigned to receive either the treatment or a placebo, considered the gold standard for testing efficacy.
A statistical analysis that combines results from multiple studies to identify patterns and draw stronger conclusions.
Neither the researchers nor the participants know who is receiving the treatment vs. placebo, reducing bias.
The study includes a group receiving an inactive substance to compare against the treatment group.
The proportion of a substance that enters circulation and is able to have an active effect.
An extract that has been processed to contain a consistent, verified amount of specific active compounds.
